name: Red Gum BBQ
address: 51 Shoreham Rd, Red Hill South VIC 3937
signature: Airy warehouse, picnic tables and low-and-slow American barbecue  -  pulled pork, brisket, smoked chicken  -  a family-friendly antidote to the winery-restaurant circuit
editorNote:
Red Gum is the Peninsula's essential non-wine-restaurant lunch. Martin Blunos and the team smoke low and slow over red gum in a big airy warehouse on the ridge, and for a decade now they have been pulling beef brisket, pork shoulder, and whole chickens out of the smokers at exactly the moment the tables are ready for them. The ordering system is counter-and-tray, the seating is picnic tables, and the vibe is the polar opposite of the hatted dining room across the road.

What makes it work beyond the smoker is the discipline. The sides are actually good  -  slaw with enough bite, pit beans cooked proper, corn bread that has not been sitting. The kids' menu exists. The beer taps rotate through Peninsula breweries. A dark and stormy arrives in a jar big enough to settle an afternoon.

Book for a crowd; go casual; come hungry. This is the antidote the Peninsula needs to its own more serious instincts, and the reason visitors keep coming back.
